text:
REGIME — Risk-on rotation: growth and precious metals led, energy sold hard.

INDICES — VIX 18.4, falling. QQQ +1.82%, IWM +1.38%, SPY +1.22%, DIA +0.70%. Small caps beat large caps on the day. Silver surged +5.51%, the standout cross-asset move, while WTI oil dropped -3.60%.

SECTORS — XLY +2.21%, XLK +1.60%, XLC +1.52%, XLRE +0.95%, XLV +0.58%, XLI +0.36%, XLF +0.23%, XLU +0.17%, XLP -0.10%, XLB -0.34%, XLE -2.03%. Top industries: WGMI +6.43%, SLV +5.51%. Bottom industries: FCG -3.30%, USO -3.60%.

THE 7s — Mag 7 +3.01%, Cloud 7 +2.42%, New Staples 7 +2.42%, Fab 7 +2.19%, Old Metals 7 +1.38%, Semi 7 +1.35%, Medtech 7 +0.98%, Buildout 7 +0.96%, New Metals 7 +0.66%, Power 7 +0.64%, Freight 7 +0.62%, Bank 7 +0.35%, Health 7 +0.34%, Industrial 7 +0.01%, Defense 7 -0.01%, Housing 7 -0.07%, Old Staples 7 -0.19%, Toll Road 7 -0.35%, Insurance 7 -0.64%, Software 7 -0.66%, Energy 7 -2.51%. Strongest in Mag 7: ORCL +4.74%. Weakest in Energy 7: WFC excluded — weakest Energy 7 component tied to oil's -3.60% drop.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29, 2026; CME shows hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%. Fed tally: 6 hawks, 5 neutral, 4 doves. Macro stance reads dovish overall with Core PCE YoY at 2.97% keeping the Fed anchored.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 60.6, Greed. Top 3 movers: ORCL +4.74% (Cloud 7), WFC -5.70% (Bank 7), SLV +5.51% (cross-asset). 20d momentum: best sector XLK +6.73%, worst XLP -3.59%; best basket Semi 7 +22.21%, worst Software 7 -12.64%. WFC dropped -5.70% on missed Q1 revenue and weak forward guidance.

WATCH — ORCL: +4.74%, 2.5x volume spike; momentum break above trend warrants follow-through check. RSPM: -0.40%, 5.4x volume spike; unusual activity in materials on a flat print signals positioning shift. GXC: +0.94%, 16.5x volume — extreme China ETF volume demands explanation given macro backdrop.
